items.title: Are We All the Same Person | Arnold Zuboff
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>What if the boundaries between persons are not ultimately real? Arnold Zuboff explores his theory of “universalism,” arguing that every conscious being may share the same underlying subject of experience — and that you and I could, in a profound sense, be the same person.

0:00 Universalism and Personhood
0:50 What Counts as a Person
3:06 Are We All the Same Person
4:49 Language and the Nature of Reality

*Arnold Zuboff* is an American philosopher known for his work on personal identity, consciousness, and probability. He took his PhD at Princeton in 2009 under Thomas Nagel and spent most of his academic career at University College London. 

Subscribe to Closer To Truth: https://www.youtube.com/@CloserToTruthTV

items.title: Theological Anthropology and the Human Mind | N.T. Wright
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>*What if Christianity never actually taught the existence of an immortal soul?* Nicholas Thomas Wright explores mind, body, resurrection, and the Holy Spirit, arguing that the New Testament rejects Platonic dualism in favor of embodied human existence renewed through resurrection.

0:00 The Mind–Body Problem and Christian Theology
1:04 Why the New Testament Has No Platonic Soul
3:00 Resurrection and the Holy Spirit
5:05 Why Only God Has Immortality
6:21 Paradise and the New Creation
7:20 Human Vocation in the Renewed Creation

Nicholas Thomas Wright is a leading English New Testament scholar, Pauline theologian, and retired Anglican bishop.

*More from Nicholas Thomas Wright on Closer To Truth:*

items.title: Choosing What Kind of Person to Become | Rebecca Newberger Goldstein
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>*What if free will matters most when we decide what kind of person we want to become?* Rebecca Goldstein explores how her idea of the “mattering instinct” relates to moral agency, identity, depression, and the possibility of libertarian free will.

0:00 Free Will and the Mattering Instinct
1:41 Compatibilism and Determinism
3:18 Choosing What Kind of Person to Become
7:32 William James and Believing in Free Will
9:09 Depression and the Feeling of Not Mattering

*Rebecca Newberger Goldstein* is a MacArthur Fellow and National Humanities Medal recipient whose ten acclaimed books span philosophy and fiction, including her new work _The Mattering Instinct: How Our Deepest Longing Drives Us and Divides Us._

*More from Rebecca Goldstein on Closer To Truth:*

items.title: What Is Human Mind in a Theistic World | Louis Caruana
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>* What happens to the human mind if God exists?* Louis Caruana argues that mind, soul, and body are not separate entities but dimensions of a single human individual — challenging both strong dualism and reductive materialism while reframing what could survive death.

0:00 Human Mind in a Theistic World
1:22 Mind, Soul, and the Individual
4:33 Theism and Human Dignity
5:17 Why Death Remains Tragic
6:36 Resurrection and the Immortal Soul

Louis Caruana SJ is a Jesuit priest ordained in 1991 and holds degrees in science, philosophy, and theology. He obtained his PhD from the University of Cambridge, and is now Dean of Philosophy at the Gregorian University, Rome, and Research Associate of Heythrop College, University of London.

*More from Louis Caruana on Closer To Truth:*

items.title: Why Is Music So Compelling?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Why does music grip us so deeply? Carl Plantinga reflects on one of the great mysteries of human experience — and how setting a psalm to music transforms it, embedding meaning in memory in ways we don't fully understand.

*Subscribe to Closer To Truth:* https://www.youtube.com/@CloserToTruthTV

#Music #CarlPlantinga #CloserToTruth</article>

items.title: Can AI Become a Person | Roman Yampolskiy
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>*Can artificial intelligence become a person, and what would that even mean?* Roman Yampolskiy argues that personhood is not an intrinsic property, but a social status assigned by humans, raising difficult questions about AI rights, consciousness, identity, and the nature of the self itself.

0:00 AI and the Question of Personhood
1:25 The Turing Test and AI Recognition
2:33 Rights Without Citizenship
3:48 AI, Selves, and Personal Identity
5:08 Why There May Be No Self

*Roman V. Yampolskiy* is a tenured Associate Professor of Computer Science at the University of Louisville's Speed School of Engineering, where he founded and directs the Cyber Security Lab. Widely credited with coining the term "AI safety" in 2011, he is the author of _AI: Unexplainable, Unpredictable, Uncontrollable._

More from Roman Yampolskiy on Closer To Truth:
* Website: https://closertotruth.com/contributor/roman-yampolskiy/

items.title: Human Nature and Human Uniqueness | Nathan Lents
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>*What makes humans unique, and are we really as different from other animals as we imagine?* Nathan Lents argues that humans share the same drives and emotions as other animals, but possess an unusual capacity to imagine alternative futures and intentionally reshape the world around them.

0:00 Human Nature and Human Uniqueness
0:44 Creating Inner Worlds
1:55 Human Nature Versus the Human Condition
3:02 Humans as Niche Constructors
5:36 Imagining Alternative Futures

Nathan H. Lents is an American scientist, author, and university professor. He has been on the faculty of John Jay College since 2006 and is currently the director of the Cell and Molecular Biology program and the former head of the honors program and the campus Macaulay Honors College program.

*More from Nathan Lents on Closer To Truth:*

items.title: What if Aliens are Too Smart for Us?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Daniel Whiteson imagines the most frustrating scenario in science — aliens arrive with all the answers, and we just can't get it.

*Subscribe to Closer To Truth:* https://www.youtube.com/@CloserToTruthTV

#Aliens #DanielWhiteson #CloserToTruth</article>

items.title: Alien Physics and the Limits of Human Knowledge | Daniel Whiteson
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>*What if human physics is only one possible way of describing reality?* Daniel Whiteson explores whether alien civilizations could possess radically different forms of physics, whether the laws of nature are discovered or invented, and whether the human mind may face fundamental limits in understanding reality.

0:00 Alien Physics and Ultimate Reality
1:42 Physics Between the Domains of Science
3:00 Epistemology and Ontology
5:10 Multiple Truths About Reality
6:03 Physics as Maps Rather Than Territory
8:10 The Limits of Human Understanding
9:33 Could Aliens Understand More Than Humans?
12:05 Quantum Mechanics and Alien Minds
13:14 Aliens as a Thought Experiment

Daniel Ofir Whiteson is an American experimental particle physicist and professor of Physics and Astronomy at University of California, Irvine. He earned a bachelor's degree in physics and computer science from Rice University and graduated with a PhD in physics from University of California, Berkeley.

items.title: Why Panentheism Misunderstands Classical Theism | Sarah Coakley
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>*What if the conflict between classical theism and panentheism is based on a misunderstanding of God itself?* Sarah Coakley argues that classical Christian theology does not portray God as distant, static, or emotionally detached, but as infinitely active and more intimately present to creation than we are to ourselves.

0:00 Panentheism and the Search for God
1:25 Misunderstanding Classical Theism
2:27 God as Infinite Action
3:41 The Reaction Against Perfect Being Theology
5:05 Science, Miracles, and Panentheism
6:00 God Beyond the God of the Gaps
7:40 Why Panentheism and Classical Theism Converge

Sarah Anne Coakley FBA is an English Anglican priest, systematic theologian, and philosopher of religion with interdisciplinary interests.

*More from Sarah Coackley on Closer To Truth:*

items.title: What If Your Brain Was Cut in Half?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>What happens when you sever the connection between the brain's two hemispheres? Arnold Zuboff explores the philosophy of brain bisection — where two distinct streams of experience appear to coexist within a single skull. The left and right hands can act with different "wants." The self, once assumed unified, begins to look like a construction. What does this mean for consciousness, personal identity, and the nature of the "I"?

Subscribe to Closer To Truth: https://www.youtube.com/@CloserToTruthTV 

#ArnoldZuboff #CloserToTruth</article>

items.title: The Eternal Now in Advaita Vedanta | Michael James
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>*What if liberation is not entering another world, but awakening from the illusion of all worlds?* Michael James explains how Advaita Vedanta and the teachings of Sri Ramana Maharshi reinterpret eternal life, liberation, time, individuality, and the end of the world as appearances within the dream of ego.

0:00 Eschatology and Liberation
1:30 Liberation Beyond Heavenly Worlds
5:07 We Are Always Liberated
7:41 The Beginning of the Illusion
10:29 Universal Liberation
12:47 The External World and the Dreamer
15:41 One Dreamer and the End of the Dream

*Michael James* has spent nearly 50 years studying, practicing, and translating the works of Sri Ramana Maharshi. He studied under Sri Sadhu Om, a direct disciple of Ramana, and is the author of _Atma-Vidya: The Science of Knowing Oneself._ He is regarded as a leading authority on Ramana's primary sources and experiential practice.

*More from Michael James on Closer To Truth:*

items.title: Why Study Theology? | Rodney Holder
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>*Can studying theology deepen faith rather than weaken it?*
After training in physics and working in science for decades, Rodney Holder turned to theology to examine Christianity analytically — including biblical contradictions, church history, philosophy of religion, and the search for God.

0:00 Why Study Theology
1:10 Biblical Criticism and Faith
4:04 Theology Beyond Literalism
4:49 Church History and the Reformation
6:43 Philosophy of Religion and Science
7:07 Theology for Ordinary Believers

The Revd Dr. Rodney Holder was Course Director of the Faraday Institute from its inception in 2006 until 2013, and remains a Bye Fellow of St. Edmund’s College and a Faraday Associate.

items.title: How Personal Identity Persists Through Time | Arnold Zuboff
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>*What makes an experience yours?* Arnold Zuboff argues that universalism is neutral toward materialism, dualism, Buddhism, Humean bundle theory, panpsychism, and idealism — because only first-person immediacy makes experience "mine."

0:00 Universalism and Theories of the Self
1:27 Universalism and Physicalism
5:29 The Electronic Corpus Callosum
9:06 Separate Experiences and One Subject
13:16 Parfit and Psychological Continuity
17:35 Buddhism and the No-Self View
22:21 Hume and the Bundle Theory
24:57 Substance Dualism and Souls
29:15 God, Souls, and the Hard Game
32:50 Panpsychism, Idealism, and Immediacy

*Arnold Zuboff* is an American philosopher known for his work on personal identity, consciousness, and probability. He took his PhD at Princeton in 2009 under Thomas Nagel and spent most of his academic career at University College London.

items.title: How Will Scientists Test Theories of Consciousness? | Lucia Melloni
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>*Can consciousness finally be studied with rigorous science?*
Neuroscientist Lucia Melloni traces how consciousness research evolved from a taboo subject into an experimental field — and explains why adversarial collaborations may help test competing theories of consciousness over the next decade.

0:00 Consciousness Enters Experimental Science
1:36 The Rise of Neural Correlates of Consciousness
3:03 Competing Theories of Consciousness
4:20 Adversarial Collaborations Between Theories
5:37 Testing Consciousness with Shared Methods
7:26 Why More Theories May Emerge
8:13 The Limits of Current Consciousness Research

Lucia Melloni leads the Neural Circuits, Consciousness, and Cognition Research Group at the Max Planck Institute for Empirical Aesthetics in Frankfurt, and holds a position as Research Professor in the Department of Neurology at NYU Grossman School of Medicine.

*More from Lucia Melloni on Closer To Truth:*
